pattern_recognition: Array Manipulation, because we are dealing with an array of digits representing a large integer. The problem signals a need for handling carry operations when incrementing the number.

approaches.title: Brute Force
approaches.intuition: Convert the array to a number, add one, and convert back to an array.
approaches.algorithm: 1. Convert the array of digits to a single integer. 2. Add one to the integer. 3. Convert the result back to an array of digits.
approaches.code_python:
from typing import List

def plusOne(digits: List[int]) -> List[int]:
    number = int(''.join(map(str, digits)))
    number += 1
    return [int(d) for d in str(number)]

approaches.title: Optimal Approach
approaches.intuition: Iterate through the array from the last digit to the first, handling carry as needed without converting to an integer.
approaches.algorithm: 1. Start from the last digit and add one. 2. If the result is 10, set the current digit to 0 and carry over 1 to the next digit. 3. Continue until all digits are processed or there is no carry left. 4. If there is still a carry after the first digit, insert 1 at the beginning of the array.

dry_run: Example input: [1, 2, 3]. Start from the last digit (3). Add 1 to it, resulting in 4. The array becomes [1, 2, 4]. No carry is generated, so the final output is [1, 2, 4].
edge_cases: Empty input should return [1]. Single element input like [9] should result in [1, 0]. Duplicates like [1, 1, 1] should become [1, 1, 2]. Large input like [9, 9, 9] should handle carry correctly to become [1, 0, 0, 0].
common_pitfalls: 1. Forgetting to handle the carry for the most significant digit can lead to incorrect results. 2. Not considering the case of an empty input array. 3. Incorrectly converting the array to an integer can cause overflow issues.
